learnings: In wrangler dev, containers aren't enabled by default - need LOCAL_DEV env var or fallback logic
getSandbox() can throw synchronously if containers not enabled - need try/catch around it
Container server runs on port 8085, worker dev on 8788 (per AGENTS.md)
Error handling: catch container errors and fall back to local dev container server
notes: JUNK/ has full SvelteKit UI - use as visual reference
JUNK2/ has Effect patterns - use as code reference
@cloudflare/sandbox is the key binding - getSandbox(), wsConnect()
ttyd needs size message {columns,rows} to start outputting
WS-001 completed: Added LOCAL_DEV to wrangler.dev.json, enhanced getSandboxResponse error handling, added fallback in /test/container endpoint

WS-002 completed: Updated /terminal/:id/start endpoint to use LOCAL_DEV check, implements sandbox.startProcess('ttyd -W -p 7681 bash') and waitForPort(7681) with 30s timeout. Works in both local dev (returns success) and production (actually starts ttyd)

WS-003 completed: Updated /terminal/:id/ws endpoint to use LOCAL_DEV pattern, uses sandbox.wsConnect(wsRequest, 7681) to connect to ttyd, returns 101 with webSocket property, calls ttydWs.accept(). Has error handling with fallback to local dev WebSocket proxy
WS-004 completed: Bidirectional message flow already implemented - sends {columns:80,rows:24} size message, forwards client→ttyd and ttyd→client messages directly without modification

FE-001 completed: Added minimal frontend - GET / returns HTML page with xterm.js, manually handles ttyd protocol (sends terminal size, handles binary messages with CMD_OUTPUT), connects to WebSocket, handles resize events. Uses same ttyd protocol handling as JUNK for compatibility.
